title: Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan
summary: A patient-specific continuity card for clinic closure, transport failure, evacuation, records, medicines, diet and fluid instructions, and symptoms requiring urgent care.
content:
A patient-specific continuity card for clinic closure, transport failure, evacuation, records, medicines, diet and fluid instructions, and symptoms requiring urgent care.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: clinician-approved emergency diet and fluid instructions
steps: Ask the dialysis team in advance for a written disaster plan, alternate centres, transport options, access-care rules, and personal symptom thresholds.
When disruption is possible, contact the usual clinic early, confirm the next treatment, and do not travel to an alternate centre without acceptance.
Carry treatment records, identification, medicines in original packaging, access information, allergies, recent laboratory information if provided, and the approved emergency instructions.
Seek urgent medical help for severe shortness of breath, chest pain, confusion, fainting, major swelling, uncontrolled bleeding, or access infection or failure.
warnings: Do not invent fluid, salt, potassium, medicine, or treatment changes.
Protect the dialysis access and never allow untrained use of it.
common_mistakes: Starting Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Dialysis disruption go-bag and early contact plan.']
